issue.title: Dependency graph UX papercuts: auto-fit, node hover flicker, criteria vanish after completion

successCriteria.description: After an issue ships (plan moved to plans/archive/), clicking its dependency-graph node shows the success-criteria checklist (rendered all-verified) instead of 'No success criteria' — get_plan falls back to plans/archive/issue-N.json

The dependency graph auto-fits to view on first node populate and when the selected release / node-id set changes, without yanking the viewport during live status-only updates; the manual Fit View button still works

Hovering a node no longer re-renders every node's internals — IssueNode is memoized so only CSS class (lineage/dimmed) changes; the hover-to-trace lineage highlight still works. (Visual: confirmed in tauri:dev.)
pnpm build clean (tsc -b), desktop vitest green, cargo test --lib green including a new get_plan archive-fallback unit test

phases.title: get_plan archive fallback (Rust) + unit test
phases.content: In apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands.rs get_plan: after the resilient read of plans/issue-N.json, if the result is Ok(None), read plans/archive/issue-N.json with the same read_json_resilient helper and return that. Active plan takes precedence; archive is the fallback (mirrors the load_tiki_releases includeArchived / read_release_changelog archive pattern). The archived plan retains phases[].status:'completed' + successCriteria + coverageMatrix, so the existing deriveCriteriaChecklist renders all criteria verified with no UI change. Add a Rust unit test in the existing commands.rs tests module: write a temp .tiki/plans/archive/issue-N.json (no active plan), assert get_plan returns Some with the criteria; and assert active plan wins when both exist.
verification: get_plan returns the archived plan when only plans/archive/issue-N.json exists
active plans/issue-N.json still takes precedence when both exist
cargo test --lib passes including the new test
phases.summary: get_plan now reads plans/issue-N.json first and falls back to plans/archive/issue-N.json on Ok(None) — shipped issues' archived plans (which retain phases[].status + successCriteria + coverageMatrix) feed the #257 success-criteria panel, which then renders all-verified instead of empty. Active plan wins when both exist. Added Rust unit test get_plan_falls_back_to_archive_for_shipped_issues (archive-only returns Some; active wins; neither = Ok(None)). cargo --lib 52→53.

phases.title: Auto fitView on load / node-set change
phases.content: In apps/desktop/src/components/dependencies/DependencyGraph.tsx add a useEffect that calls fitView({ padding: 0.2, duration: 300 }) when the node-id set changes. Derive a stable nodeIdSignature = useMemo(nodes.map(n=>n.id).sort().join(','), [nodes]); key the effect on [nodeIdSignature, fitView]. This fits on first populate ('' -> ids) and on release switch (signature changes) but NOT on live status-only updates or hover (node-id set unchanged -> same signature string -> effect skipped), so it never fights a mid-pan user. Wrap the fitView call in requestAnimationFrame so nodes are measured first; cancel on cleanup. Keep the manual Fit View button and the existing fitView prop.
verification: effect keyed on a stable node-id signature, not on the nodes array reference
graph frames itself on first load and on release switch
no auto-fit on hover or live status update (signature unchanged)
phases.summary: Added a useEffect keyed on a stable nodeIdSignature (sorted joined node ids) that calls fitView({padding:0.2,duration:300}) inside requestAnimationFrame. Fires on first populate and release switch (signature changes) but NOT on live status-only updates or hover (same id set → same signature → effect skipped), so it never yanks the viewport mid-pan. Manual Fit View button + existing fitView prop retained. tsc -b clean.

phases.title: Memoize IssueNode to kill hover flicker
phases.content: In apps/desktop/src/components/dependencies/IssueNode.tsx wrap the component in React.memo. On hover the graph re-derives styledNodes (a fresh array with new className per node) but preserves each node's data reference; memoizing IssueNode means className-only changes (lineage/dimmed, applied by ReactFlow on the node WRAPPER) no longer re-render each node's inner JSX, removing the per-hover re-render storm that causes the visible blink. The lineage highlight (pure CSS opacity/box-shadow) still works. INTERACTIVE: final blink-gone confirmation requires tauri:dev.
verification: IssueNode wrapped in React.memo; nodeTypes still defined at module scope
phases.summary: Wrapped IssueNode in React.memo (extracted IssueNodeComponent + export const IssueNode = memo(...)). On hover the graph mints a fresh styledNodes array with new per-node className but preserves each node's data ref, so the memoized inner component no longer re-renders on className-only changes — removing the per-hover re-render storm behind the blink. Lineage/dimmed highlight is pure-CSS on the wrapper, unchanged. tsc -b clean. SC3 blink-gone is VISUAL — needs user tauri:dev confirmation.
